sections.paragraph:
νῶτον (νῶτον, ου, τό,)

I. the back, Lat. tergum, Il.; often in pl., like Lat. terga, Hom.; τὰ νῶτα ἐντρέπειν, ἐπιστρέφειν to turn the back, i. e. flee, Hdt.; νῶτα δεῖξαι Plut.; κατὰ νώτου from behind, in rear, Hdt., Thuc.


Etym. pl. always νῶτα, τά

II. metaph. any wide surface, ἐπ' εὐρέα νῶτα θαλάσσης Hom.; of plains, Pind., Eur.

2. the back or ridge, of a hill, Pind., Eur.; of a chariot, Eur.
